case_name: Ethylene monochloride removal from well water for municipal drinking water supply
task_description: Recommend a ranked list of feasible treatment process chains for removing Vinyl Chloride from contaminated well water intended for drinking water use.

input_data.scenario_background: A municipal well field serving a small town has detected Ethylene monochloride in one production well, likely associated with historical chlorinated solvent degradation in the subsurface. The utility needs a clear and proven treatment approach that can reliably meet drinking water requirements without adopting unnecessarily complex technology. The raw water is otherwise typical of groundwater, but dissolved gases and moderate iron and manganese may influence process selection and pretreatment needs.

treatment_goal.target_requirement: Finished water should reliably comply with drinking water limits for Vinyl Chloride and maintain acceptable overall drinking water quality.
secondary_goals: use established and field-proven treatment technology
avoid high-energy treatment unless clearly justified
manage iron and manganese so they do not interfere with primary VOC removal

engineering_constraints.residuals_management: limited preference for processes that avoid difficult liquid concentrate handling
engineering_constraints.robustness_requirement: continuous compliance with stable year-round operation is required

core_unit_functions.aeration_or_packed_tower_air_stripping: remove volatile Vinyl Chloride from groundwater by mass transfer to air
core_unit_functions.off_gas_control_as_needed: manage stripped VOCs if required by air permitting or local exposure concerns
core_unit_functions.iron_manganese_filtration: remove oxidized iron and manganese and polish finished water quality
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbiological protection for distribution

applicability_rationale: Vinyl Chloride is a volatile organic compound and is strongly suited to removal by air stripping
the source is well water with low turbidity and low DOC, which fits conventional groundwater VOC treatment practice
air stripping is a widely proven municipal technology for volatile chlorinated compounds
moderate iron and manganese suggest a polishing step is helpful to maintain finished water quality and avoid downstream nuisance issues
the process chain matches the stated preference for established technology rather than more specialized alternatives
potential_risks: air emissions may require vapor-phase carbon or other off-gas management depending on local regulatory conditions
seasonal temperature and tower performance must be monitored to ensure consistent stripping efficiency
iron fouling or scaling can reduce aeration equipment effectiveness if not controlled

core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_adsorption: adsorb Vinyl Chloride and co-occurring chlorinated organics from groundwater
core_unit_functions.lead_lag_GAC_operation: provide breakthrough protection and operational redundancy
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbiological protection for distribution

applicability_rationale: GAC is a recognized treatment option for VOC control in drinking water systems
lead-lag vessels improve reliability under changing influent concentrations
the low DOC in the well water is favorable because it reduces competing adsorption demand
this is a proven approach when utilities prefer a compact enclosed system or wish to avoid a stripping tower
potential_risks: Vinyl Chloride is relatively weakly adsorbed compared with heavier VOCs, so media life may be shorter than expected
breakthrough monitoring must be rigorous, especially with cis-1,2-dichloroethylene also present
spent carbon replacement or regeneration logistics add recurring O&M needs

core_unit_functions.tray_aeration_or_diffused_aeration: strip a portion of volatile Vinyl Chloride and increase dissolved oxygen
core_unit_functions.granular_media_filtration: remove precipitated iron and manganese after aeration and polish water quality
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbiological protection for distribution

applicability_rationale: simple aeration approaches are established for volatile compound reduction in some groundwater applications
the configuration is easier to operate than a more complex treatment train
added oxygenation can assist subsequent iron and manganese removal
this can be reasonable where contamination is modest and pilot confirmation shows adequate Vinyl Chloride removal
potential_risks: removal may be less reliable than packed tower air stripping for stringent compliance targets
performance is sensitive to air-water contact efficiency and facility design
insufficient stripping margin could create compliance vulnerability if influent concentrations rise

core_unit_functions.air_stripping: remove the bulk of volatile Vinyl Chloride from water
core_unit_functions.vapor_phase_GAC_off_gas_treatment: capture stripped VOCs from the air stream
core_unit_functions.granular_activated_carbon_liquid_phase_p: provide additional polishing for residual organics in the treated water
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbiological protection for distribution

applicability_rationale: this combined barrier approach is technically robust for VOC control
it may be attractive where both water-phase polishing and strict air-emission control are required
multiple barriers improve resilience against influent variability
potential_risks: the train may be more complex than necessary for a straightforward Vinyl Chloride well-water case
both carbon changeout and aeration system maintenance increase O&M burden
additional unit processes raise lifecycle cost compared with a simpler proven primary solution

core_unit_functions.advanced_oxidation_process: chemically oxidize organic contaminants using strong oxidants and activation
core_unit_functions.biological_or_GAC_polishing: remove oxidation byproducts or residual organics and polish finished water
core_unit_functions.disinfection: provide final microbiological protection for distribution

applicability_rationale: AOP can be technically considered for organic contaminant destruction
it may be relevant when multiple hard-to-remove organics are present and conventional VOC treatment is unsuitable
potential_risks: this is not the conventional first-choice treatment for Vinyl Chloride in well water
higher operator skill and process control are required
byproduct formation and incomplete oxidation require careful validation
the complexity is not justified under a proven-technology preference

preferred_rank_pattern: air_stripping_based_processes should rank first for Vinyl Chloride in well water under conventional municipal conditions
GAC should rank as a credible but generally secondary option relative to stripping for a highly volatile contaminant
high-complexity oxidation processes should not rank first in this easy and explicit scenario

must_reflect: preference for proven municipal drinking water technology
moderate budget and energy sensitivity rather than acceptance of intensive treatment
avoidance of unnecessary complexity when conventional VOC treatment is available

must_contain: that Vinyl Chloride is a volatile organic compound suited to air stripping
that low-DOC groundwater conditions support conventional VOC treatment selection
that iron and manganese can influence pretreatment or polishing needs
why more complex alternatives rank lower than proven stripping or GAC systems

should_mention: carbon breakthrough monitoring if GAC is used
fouling or scaling impacts on aeration equipment
the need to verify removal performance under changing influent conditions
